standard_full_name: Machine Learning Leakage Guard — Prediction Model Integrity Standard

citation.preferred_citation: Machine Learning Leakage Guard (MLGG) Standard v1.0. ml-leakage-guard project. 2026. https://github.com/Furinaaa-Cancan/medical-ml-leakage-guard
citation.note: When citing this standard in a manuscript, please include the MLGG version number and the conformance level achieved (e.g., 'MLGG v1.0 L3-Publication-Grade').

scope.description: The MLGG Standard defines a set of machine-verifiable requirements for the development, validation, and reporting of clinical prediction models. It specifies 31 audit gates organized in a directed acyclic graph (DAG) that a model development pipeline must satisfy to achieve a given conformance level.
in_scope: Supervised machine learning models for clinical prediction
Models intended for publication in peer-reviewed journals
out_of_scope_v1: Natural language processing / clinical text models

planned_extensions.v2.0: Imaging, NLP, and genomics domain packs via plugin architecture

normative_references.name: TRIPOD+AI 2024
normative_references.citation: Collins GS, Moons KGM, Dhiman P, et al. BMJ. 2024;385:e078378.

normative_references.name: PROBAST+AI 2025
normative_references.citation: Wolff RF, Moons KGM, Riley RD, et al. PROBAST+AI. BMJ. 2025.
normative_references.role: Risk of bias assessment — 34 signalling questions across 4 domains

normative_references.name: STARD-AI 2021
normative_references.citation: Sounderajah V, et al. BMJ Open. 2021;11:e047709.

normative_references.name: Riley EPV Formula
normative_references.role: Sample size requirements — events per variable calculation

normative_references.name: Vickers DCA 2006
normative_references.citation: Vickers AJ, Elkin EB. Med Decis Making. 2006;26:565–574.
normative_references.role: Clinical utility — decision curve analysis requirement

terminology.prediction_model: A mathematical function that uses participant characteristics (predictors) to estimate the probability or value of a future outcome.
terminology.data_leakage: Any process by which information from outside the training data is used to create a model, leading to overly optimistic performance estimates.
terminology.gate: A self-contained audit script that validates a specific aspect of the prediction pipeline. Gates are fail-closed: they exit 0 on pass, 2 on fail.
terminology.strict_mode: Pipeline execution mode in which warnings are treated as failures. Required for L3-Publication-Grade conformance.
terminology.conformance_level: A classification of a pipeline's compliance with MLGG requirements. Three levels: L1, L2, L3.
terminology.evidence_artifact: A machine-generated JSON file (gate report) that constitutes cryptographic evidence of a gate's pass/fail outcome.
terminology.compliance_certificate: A signed, bundled artifact containing all gate reports and a conformance level declaration, suitable for submission alongside a manuscript.
terminology.index_time: The time point at which a prediction is made. All predictor values must be available at or before this time.
terminology.fail_closed: The principle that any ambiguous or error state results in a gate failure rather than a silent pass.

L1.name: Leakage-Audited
L1.description: The pipeline has passed all critical data integrity and anti-leakage gates. Results can be presented at conferences with appropriate caveats.
L1.use_context: Conference papers, preliminary reports, internal validation studies

L2.name: Statistically Valid
L2.description: The pipeline has passed all L1 gates plus statistical validation gates. Results can be submitted to specialist journals with the MLGG L2 compliance certificate.
L2.use_context: Specialist journals (JAMIA, J Biomed Inform, npj Digital Medicine), internal clinical validation

L3.name: Publication-Grade
L3.description: The pipeline has passed all 31 gates in strict mode. Results meet the standards required for top-tier journals including Nature Medicine, Lancet Digital Health, JAMA, and BMJ.
L3.use_context: Nature Medicine, Lancet Digital Health, JAMA, BMJ, New England Journal of Medicine

gate_dag.description: The 31 gates are organized in a DAG with 9 execution layers. Gates in the same layer can execute in parallel. Each gate must complete before gates that depend on it.

0_contract.description: Validates and normalizes request.json — all subsequent gates depend on this

1_manifest.description: SHA-256 cryptographic integrity verification of all input files

2_attestation.description: Cryptographic execution timestamp and witness verification

3_data_validation.description: Core data integrity and leakage detection

5_model_audit.description: Model selection and feature engineering compliance

7_aggregation.description: Aggregates all gate outcomes into a publication readiness assessment

8_final.description: 10-dimension quality scoring and cryptographic model signing

scoring_rubric.description: 10-dimension scoring rubric for quantitative evaluation. Maximum score: 100 points. Minimum for L3 conformance: 90/100.

D1_data_integrity.description: Split isolation, patient-level disjoint, temporal ordering, row overlap

D2_leakage_prevention.description: Target, definition-variable, lineage, and post-index leakage prevention

D3_pipeline_isolation.description: Preprocessor, imputer, and resampling train-only constraints

D4_model_selection.description: Candidate pool, one-SE rule, no test peeking, baseline comparator

D5_statistical_validity.description: Bootstrap CI, permutation test, calibration, DCA, metric consistency

D6_generalization.description: Train-test gap, external cohort, transport-drop CI, seed stability

D7_clinical_completeness.description: Full metric panel, confusion matrix consistency, threshold feasibility

D8_reporting_standards.description: TRIPOD+AI, PROBAST+AI, exclusion criteria, limitation documentation

D9_reproducibility.description: Seed logging, version tracking, execution attestation, manifest lock

D10_security_provenance.description: Model signing, artifact integrity, sensitive data protection

interpretation.75_89: Solid but gaps remain (需补充) — L2 conformance achievable
interpretation.60_74: Major issues (重大缺陷) — L1 only; significant remediation required
interpretation.below_60: Not publishable (不可发表) — fundamental methodology failures

compliance_certificate.description: A MLGG Compliance Certificate is a machine-readable, HMAC-signed JSON artifact that bundles all gate reports and attests to the conformance level. It can be submitted alongside a manuscript for independent verification.
compliance_certificate.verification: The certificate signature can be verified by running: python3 scripts/generate_compliance_certificate.py --verify <certificate.json>

change_management.major_change: Removal of required gates, reduction of conformance requirements, changes that lower the bar
change_management.minor_change: Addition of new gates, new conformance levels, new reference standards — backward compatible
change_management.deprecation_policy: Deprecated gates remain functional for 2 minor versions before removal

governance.contribution_process: See CONTRIBUTING.md for gate contribution guidelines
governance.review_cycle: Annual review of normative references; immediate patch for critical bugs
